title: Innodata (INOD) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript
text: Innodata delivered record-breaking Q1 2026 results, with revenue reaching $90.1 million, representing 54% year-over-year growth and significantly exceeding analyst expectations. The company achieved adjusted gross margin of 47% and adjusted EBITDA of $25 million, or 28% of revenue. Revenue exceeded consensus by approximately $13.6 million (18%). Adjusted EBITDA surpassed consensus by 139%. Net income of $14.9 million with diluted EPS of $0.42 versus consensus of $0.08. Cash balance increased to $117.4 million. A leading big tech customer will contribute approximately $51 million in projected 2026 revenue, making it the company's anticipated second-largest customer up from zero revenue twelve months prior. The company also expanded its Wells Fargo credit facility from $30 million to $50 million. The firm strengthened positions across frontier AI labs, federal markets, and enterprise customers. A hyperscaler selected Innodata as its global trust and safety partner with anticipated $3 million in initial annualized run-rate revenue. Revenue from other tech customers grew 453% year-over-year, demonstrating improved customer diversification. Management raised full-year 2026 revenue growth guidance to approximately 40% or more, up from 35% previously stated.
